entries.name: Uluru-Kata Tjuta National Park
entries.desc: A dual World Heritage Site in the Red Centre, protecting the vast sandstone monolith of Uluru and the domes of Kata Tjuta, both sacred to the Anangu people.

photo.alt: Uluru at sunset, Northern Territory
photo.credit: <!-- photo: uluru.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Golden Uluru Sunset.jpg" by Matheus Hobold Sovernigo · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Golden_Uluru_Sunset.jpg -->

entries.name: Kakadu National Park
entries.desc: Australia's largest national park and a dual World Heritage Site, spanning wetlands, escarpments, and waterfalls, with ancient Aboriginal rock art and abundant birdlife and crocodiles.

photo.alt: Yellow Water wetlands, Kakadu National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: kakadu-yellow-water.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Kakadu (AU), Kakadu National Park, Yellow Water -- 2019 -- 3957.jpg" by Dietmar Rabich · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Kakadu_(AU),_Kakadu_National_Park,_Yellow_Water_--_2019_--_3957.jpg -->

entries.name: Chobe National Park
entries.desc: Home to one of the greatest concentrations of elephants on Earth, the park draws tens of thousands of animals to its river floodplains in the dry season. Boat safaris along the Chobe River offer close encounters with elephants, hippos, and crocodiles.

photo.alt: Elephants gathered on the Chobe River
photo.credit: <!-- photo: chobe-elephants.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Elephants on the Chobe River.jpg" by AoneMokwena · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Elephants_on_the_Chobe_River.jpg -->

entries.name: Iguaçu National Park
entries.desc: A UNESCO site on the Brazilian side of the Iguazu Falls, one of the world's largest waterfall systems, set within subtropical rainforest shared with neighbouring Argentina.

photo.alt: Iguazu Falls, view from the Brazilian side
photo.credit: <!-- photo: iguazu-falls.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "00 1838 Iguazu Falls from the Brazilian side.jpg" by W. Bulach · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:00_1838_Iguazu_Falls_from_the_Brazilian_side.jpg -->

entries.name: Lençóis Maranhenses National Park
entries.desc: A coastal expanse of rolling white sand dunes that, during the rainy season, fills with thousands of turquoise freshwater lagoons, creating a striking seasonal landscape.

photo.alt: Sand dunes and turquoise lagoons, Lençóis Maranhenses
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lencois-maranhenses.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Dunas e lagoas nos Lençóis Maranhenses.jpg" by Bruno Lenharo · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Dunas_e_lagoas_nos_Len%C3%A7%C3%B3is_Maranhenses.jpg -->

entries.name: Banff National Park
entries.desc: Canada's oldest national park, set in the Rocky Mountains and known for turquoise glacial lakes such as Louise and Moraine, alpine peaks, and abundant wildlife.

photo.alt: Moraine Lake, Banff National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: moraine-lake-banff.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Moraine Lake-Banff NP.JPG" by Tobias Alt, Tobi 87 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Moraine_Lake-Banff_NP.JPG -->

entries.name: Jasper National Park
entries.desc: The largest national park in the Canadian Rockies, spanning icefields, glaciers, and forested valleys. It is designated a Dark Sky Preserve for exceptional stargazing.

photo.alt: Athabasca Glacier, Columbia Icefield, Jasper National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: athabasca-glacier-jasper.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Athabasca Glacier, Jasper National Park (7800641370).jpg" by Scarlet Sappho · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Athabasca_Glacier,_Jasper_National_Park_(7800641370).jpg -->

meta: Largest National Park in the Canadian Rockies

entries.name: Pacific Rim National Park Reserve
entries.desc: A rugged stretch of Vancouver Island coastline with old-growth temperate rainforest, long surf beaches, and the multi-day West Coast Trail through wild terrain.

photo.alt: Long Beach, Pacific Rim National Park Reserve
photo.credit: <!-- photo: long-beach-pacific-rim.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Long beach - Tofino - Vancouver Island - 12.jpg" by Buiobuione · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Long_beach_-_Tofino_-_Vancouver_Island_-_12.jpg -->

entries.name: Gros Morne National Park
entries.desc: A UNESCO World Heritage Site renowned for its geology, including the Tablelands where exposed mantle rock reveals plate tectonics, plus fjord-like lakes and coastal cliffs.

photo.alt: Western Brook Pond fjord-lake, Gros Morne National Park, Newfoundland
photo.credit: <!-- photo: western-brook-pond-gros-morne.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Gros Morne National Park - Western Brook Pond.jpg" by David W Enstrom · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Gros_Morne_National_Park_-_Western_Brook_Pond.jpg -->

entries.name: Torres del Paine National Park
entries.desc: A flagship of Patagonia, defined by the granite Paine towers, glacier-fed lakes, and the multi-day W and O trekking circuits through wind-scoured mountains and steppe.

photo.alt: Granite towers of Torres del Paine, Chilean Patagonia
photo.credit: <!-- photo: torres-del-paine-towers.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "All torres no clouds Torres Del Paine National Park Chile.jpg" by amanderson2 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:All_torres_no_clouds_Torres_Del_Paine_National_Park_Chile.jpg -->

entries.name: Los Glaciares National Park
entries.desc: A UNESCO site holding the Southern Patagonian Ice Field, the advancing Perito Moreno Glacier, and the jagged spires of Fitz Roy and Cerro Torre near El Chaltén.

photo.alt: Perito Moreno Glacier and Andes peaks, Los Glaciares National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: perito-moreno-glacier.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Grandeur - The Perito Moreno Glacier (Argentina) (8095666402).jpg" by Jorge Láscar · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:%22Grandeur%22_-_The_Perito_Moreno_Glacier_(Argentina)_(8095666402).jpg -->

entries.name: Zhangjiajie National Forest Park
entries.desc: China's first national forest park, famed for thousands of towering quartzite-sandstone pillars rising from misty forest, cited as an inspiration for the film Avatar.

photo.alt: Quartzite-sandstone pillar forest, Zhangjiajie National Forest Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: zhangjiajie-pillars.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Zhangjiajie National Forest Park.jpg" by Kuruman · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Zhangjiajie_National_Forest_Park.jpg -->

entries.name: Jiuzhaigou Valley National Park
entries.desc: A UNESCO valley on the edge of the Tibetan Plateau, celebrated for multicoloured mineral-rich lakes, layered waterfalls, and forests that turn vivid in autumn.

photo.alt: Multicoloured mineral lake with submerged trees, Jiuzhaigou Valley
photo.credit: <!-- photo: jiuzhaigou-valley-lake.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "One of the great wonders of China.jpg" by Bhu22 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:One_of_the_great_wonders_of_China.jpg -->

entries.name: Tayrona National Park
entries.desc: On Colombia's Caribbean coast, it combines palm-backed coves and coral reefs with tropical jungle rising toward the Sierra Nevada and archaeological remains of the Tayrona people.

photo.alt: Cabo San Juan de Guía beach, Tayrona National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: cabo-san-juan-tayrona.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Cabo San Juan de Guia.JPG" by Kasio2442 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cabo_San_Juan_de_Guia.JPG -->

entries.name: Corcovado National Park
entries.desc: Protecting one of the largest tracts of lowland tropical rainforest in Central America, it is famed for extraordinary biodiversity, including tapirs, jaguars, and scarlet macaws.

photo.alt: Palm-lined rainforest meeting the Pacific coast, Corcovado National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: corcovado-rainforest.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Beach of Costa Rica (8440070258).jpg" by rob Stoeltje · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Beach_of_Costa_Rica_(8440070258).jpg -->

entries.name: Arenal Volcano National Park
entries.desc: Centred on the near-symmetrical Arenal Volcano, once among the world's most active, the park pairs rainforest trails and hanging bridges with nearby geothermal hot springs.

photo.alt: Arenal Volcano cone rising above rainforest, Costa Rica
photo.credit: <!-- photo: arenal-volcano.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Arenal volcano. Costa Rica.jpg" by Leonora (Ellie) Enking · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Arenal_volcano._Costa_Rica.jpg -->

entries.name: Plitvice Lakes National Park
entries.desc: Croatia's oldest and best-known national park, a UNESCO site where sixteen terraced lakes linked by cascading waterfalls flow through travertine barriers and dense forest.

photo.alt: Turquoise cascade and pool at Plitvice Lakes National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: plitvice-lakes.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Croatia - Plitvice Lakes National Park.jpg" by 5snake5 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Croatia_-_Plitvice_Lakes_National_Park.jpg -->

entries.name: Galápagos National Park
entries.desc: Ecuador's first national park and a UNESCO site, this remote volcanic archipelago shaped Darwin's theory of evolution and shelters giant tortoises, marine iguanas, and endemic birds.

photo.alt: Galápagos sea lions resting on volcanic rock, Santa Fe Island
photo.credit: <!-- photo: galapagos-sea-lions.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Galápagos sea lions, Santa Fe Island 01.jpg" by Bernard Gagnon · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Gal%C3%A1pagos_sea_lions,_Santa_Fe_Island_01.jpg -->

entries.name: Berchtesgaden National Park
entries.desc: Germany's only alpine national park, centered on the emerald Königssee, one of the country's cleanest lakes, and the Watzmann massif. Ibex, chamois, and golden eagles inhabit the surrounding limestone peaks.

photo.alt: Aerial view of the Watzmann massif and Königssee lake
photo.credit: <!-- photo: watzmann-konigssee.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Aerial image of Watzmann and Königssee (view from the south).jpg" by Carsten Steger · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Aerial_image_of_Watzmann_and_K%C3%B6nigssee_(view_from_the_south).jpg -->

entries.name: Vatnajökull National Park
entries.desc: One of Europe's largest national parks, built around the vast Vatnajökull ice cap. It encompasses glaciers, active volcanoes, and seasonal blue ice caves.

photo.alt: Glowing blue ice cave within the Vatnajökull glacier
photo.credit: <!-- photo: vatnajokull-ice-cave.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Ice Cave Blue Roof - Iceland.jpg" by Eric Kilby · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Ice_Cave_Blue_Roof_-_Iceland.jpg -->

meta: One of Europe's Largest National Parks

entries.name: Þingvellir National Park
entries.desc: A UNESCO site straddling the rift between the North American and Eurasian tectonic plates, and the historic meeting place of Iceland's parliament from the year 930.

photo.alt: Rift gorge between the North American and Eurasian tectonic plates
photo.credit: <!-- photo: thingvellir-rift.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Between two Continents (15869930547).jpg" by Rob Oo from NL · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Between_two_Continents_(15869930547).jpg -->

entries.name: Ranthambore National Park
entries.desc: One of India's foremost tiger reserves, where dry deciduous forest and lakes surround the ruins of a historic hilltop fort, offering strong chances of spotting Bengal tigers.

photo.alt: Lake ruins with Ranthambore's hilltop fort in the background
photo.credit: <!-- photo: ranthambore-fort-lake.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Ranthambore lack.jpg" by Shrikantdevdatta · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Ranthambore_lack.jpg -->

entries.name: Komodo National Park
entries.desc: A UNESCO group of islands that is the only natural habitat of the Komodo dragon, the world's largest lizard, surrounded by coral reefs prized for diving and snorkelling.

photo.alt: Komodo dragon resting on the ground
photo.credit: <!-- photo: komodo-dragon.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Komodo Dragon in Komodo Island 02.jpg" by Hotel Kaesong · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Komodo_Dragon_in_Komodo_Island_02.jpg -->

entries.name: Gran Paradiso National Park
entries.desc: Italy's first national park and the only one centred entirely on a peak within Italy, Gran Paradiso at 4,061 metres. It is the stronghold of the Alpine ibex, saved from extinction here in the 19th century, alongside chamois and golden eagles.

photo.alt: Alpine ibex on a grassy slope with snow-capped peaks behind
photo.credit: <!-- photo: gran-paradiso-ibex.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Capra ibex in Gran Paradiso National Park.jpg" by Irene Vercellino · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Capra_ibex_in_Gran_Paradiso_National_Park.jpg -->

entries.name: Fuji-Hakone-Izu National Park
entries.desc: Spanning Mount Fuji, the hot-spring resorts of Hakone, the Izu Peninsula coast, and offshore islands, it is one of Japan's most visited parks and lies near Tokyo.

photo.alt: Mount Fuji reflected in Lake Kawaguchi
photo.credit: <!-- photo: mount-fuji-lake-kawaguchi.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Mount Fuji from Lake Kawaguchi s2.jpg" by Alpsdake · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mount_Fuji_from_Lake_Kawaguchi_s2.jpg -->

entries.name: Yakushima National Park
entries.desc: A subtropical island whose interior holds a UNESCO-listed ancient cedar forest, including millennia-old yakusugi trees, mossy ravines, and one of Japan's wettest climates.

photo.alt: Jomon Sugi, the ancient yakusugi cedar tree
photo.credit: <!-- photo: jomon-sugi-ancient-cedar.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "Jomon Sugi 03.jpg" by Σ64 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Jomon_Sugi_03.jpg -->

entries.name: Wadi Rum Protected Area
entries.desc: A UNESCO desert of towering sandstone and granite formations, natural arches, and ancient inscriptions, long inhabited by Bedouin and famous as a film-location backdrop.

photo.alt: Sandstone desert formations of Wadi Rum
photo.credit: <!-- photo: wadi-rum-desert.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Wadi Rum Desert 01.jpg" by BarabaszO · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Wadi_Rum_Desert_01.jpg -->

entries.name: Amboseli National Park
entries.desc: One of Africa's most photogenic parks, where large herds of free-roaming elephants move against the iconic backdrop of Mount Kilimanjaro rising across the border in Tanzania.

photo.alt: Elephants at Amboseli National Park with Mount Kilimanjaro behind
photo.credit: <!-- photo: amboseli-elephants-kilimanjaro.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Elephants at Amboseli national park against Mount Kilimanjaro.jpg" by Amoghavarsha JS · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Elephants_at_Amboseli_national_park_against_Mount_Kilimanjaro.jpg -->

entries.name: Maasai Mara National Reserve
entries.desc: Kenya's most celebrated wildlife reserve, sharing the Serengeti ecosystem with Tanzania. It is among the world's best locations to witness lion, cheetah, and leopard, and the dramatic wildebeest river crossings of the annual Great Migration.

photo.alt: Wildebeest migration across the Maasai Mara
photo.credit: <!-- photo: maasai-mara-wildebeest-migration.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Wildebeest Migration Masai mara.jpg" by Nature Exploration Safaris · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Wildebeest_Migration_Masai_mara.jpg -->

entries.name: Kinabalu National Park
entries.desc: A UNESCO site centered on Mount Kinabalu, the highest peak in Southeast Asia at 4,095 metres, with exceptional botanical diversity including carnivorous pitcher plants and hundreds of orchid species. Summit treks require a permit and overnight stay.

photo.alt: Mount Kinabalu, the highest peak in Southeast Asia
photo.credit: <!-- photo: mount-kinabalu.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Mount Kinabalu 1.jpg" by ReubenST · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mount_Kinabalu_1.jpg -->

entries.name: Gunung Mulu National Park
entries.desc: A UNESCO karst landscape in Sarawak's rainforest, home to Deer Cave — one of the world's largest cave passages by volume — and the razor-edged Pinnacles rock formation, reached by a two-day jungle trek from the river.

photo.alt: The Pinnacles limestone karst spires of Gunung Mulu
photo.credit: <!-- photo: mulu-pinnacles.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Pinnacles at Mulu.jpg" by Paul White · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Pinnacles_at_Mulu.jpg -->

entries.name: Etosha National Park
entries.desc: Built around a huge salt pan visible from space, this reserve draws elephants, rhinos, and big cats to its waterholes, where wildlife gathers in the dry season.

photo.alt: Wildlife gathered at a waterhole in Etosha National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: etosha-chudop-waterhole.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Etosha National Park, Chudop waterhole.jpg" by Olga Ernst · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Etosha_National_Park,_Chudop_waterhole.jpg -->

entries.name: Sagarmatha National Park
entries.desc: A UNESCO Himalayan park encompassing the Nepalese side of Mount Everest and other high peaks, home to Sherpa communities and the classic trek to Everest Base Camp.

photo.alt: Ama Dablam and Mount Everest rising above the Himalayas
photo.credit: <!-- photo: sagarmatha-ama-dablam-everest.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Ama dablam and Everest.jpg" by Sangyam adventurer · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Ama_dablam_and_Everest.jpg -->

entries.name: Fiordland National Park
entries.desc: New Zealand's largest national park, a UNESCO wilderness of deep glacier-carved fiords such as Milford and Doubtful Sound, waterfalls, and celebrated Great Walks.

photo.alt: Milford Sound fiord with Mitre Peak, Fiordland National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: fiordland-milford-sound-mitre-peak.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "A moody Milford Sound. NZ (26721630361).jpg" by Bernard Spragg. NZ · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:A_moody_Milford_Sound._NZ_(26721630361).jpg -->

entries.name: Tongariro National Park
entries.desc: New Zealand's oldest national park and a dual World Heritage Site for nature and Maori culture, home to three active volcanoes and the renowned Tongariro Alpine Crossing.

photo.alt: Mount Tongariro and Mount Ngauruhoe volcanoes
photo.credit: <!-- photo: tongariro-ngauruhoe-volcanoes.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Mount Tongariro and Ngauruhoe.jpg" by Javier Sánchez Portero · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mount_Tongariro_and_Ngauruhoe.jpg -->

entries.name: Jotunheimen National Park
entries.desc: Home to Norway's highest peaks, including Galdhøpiggen, this mountain park offers glaciers, deep valleys, and the famous Besseggen ridge hike above two lakes.

photo.alt: Besseggen ridge between Lake Gjende and Lake Bessvatnet
photo.credit: <!-- photo: jotunheimen-besseggen-ridge.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Bessvatnet-Besseggen-Gjende.jpg" by Xauxa (Håkan Svensson) · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bessvatnet-Besseggen-Gjende.jpg -->

entries.name: Lofoten Islands
entries.desc: An Arctic archipelago where steep granite peaks rise straight from the sea beside fishing villages and white beaches, known for midnight sun and winter aurora.

photo.alt: Reine fishing village beneath granite peaks, Lofoten Islands
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lofoten-reine-village.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Reine - Lofoten.jpg" by Jakub Fryš · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Reine_-_Lofoten.jpg -->

entries.name: Huascarán National Park
entries.desc: A UNESCO reserve in the Cordillera Blanca, the highest tropical mountain range on Earth, with glaciated peaks over 6,000 metres, alpine lakes, and rare Puya raimondii plants.

photo.alt: Huascarán glacier peak above the Llanganuco valley, Cordillera Blanca
photo.credit: <!-- photo: huascaran-llanganuco-valley.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Huascarán desde quebrada Llanganuco.jpg" by Dardanadardana · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Huascar%C3%A1n_desde_quebrada_Llanganuco.jpg -->

entries.name: Volcanoes National Park
entries.desc: One of the premier destinations for gorilla trekking, where small permitted groups track endangered mountain gorilla families through dense bamboo forest on the slopes of the Virunga volcanic chain, shared with Uganda and DRC.

photo.alt: Mountain gorilla in the bamboo forest of Volcanoes National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: volcanoes-np-rwanda-mountain-gorilla.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "A mountain gorilla from Susa Group in Karisimbi thicket of Volcanoes National Park in Rwanda. Emmanuel Kwizera.jpg" by Emmanuelkwizera · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:A_mountain_gorilla_from_Susa_Group_in_Karisimbi_thicket_of_Volcanoes_National_Park_in_Rwanda._Emmanuel_Kwizera.jpg -->

entries.name: Kruger National Park
entries.desc: One of Africa's largest game reserves and a premier Big Five destination, with an extensive network of roads and camps that make self-drive safaris widely accessible.

photo.alt: African elephant in the bushveld of Kruger National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: kruger-elephant.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "An elephant in Kruger National Park.jpg" by Dario Crespi · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:An_elephant_in_Kruger_National_Park.jpg -->

entries.name: Table Mountain National Park
entries.desc: Wrapping Cape Town and the Cape Peninsula, it takes in the flat-topped Table Mountain, the Cape of Good Hope, rich fynbos vegetation, and a resident African penguin colony.

photo.alt: Table Mountain rising above Cape Town
photo.credit: <!-- photo: table-mountain-cape-town.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Cape Town Table Mountain.jpg" by Matthiasmullie · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cape_Town_Table_Mountain.jpg -->

entries.name: Teide National Park
entries.desc: A UNESCO volcanic landscape built around Mount Teide, Spain's highest peak, with lava fields and calderas. Clear skies make it a leading site for astronomical observation.

photo.alt: Mount Teide volcano rising above the caldera on Tenerife
photo.credit: <!-- photo: mount-teide-tenerife.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Mount Teide Tenerife IMGP2085.jpg" by Nikodem Nijaki · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mount_Teide_Tenerife_IMGP2085.jpg -->

entries.name: Ordesa y Monte Perdido
entries.desc: One of Spain's oldest national parks, in the central Pyrenees, famous for deep glacial canyons, limestone cirques, and waterfalls beneath the Monte Perdido massif.

photo.alt: Glacial valley and forested slopes of Ordesa in the central Pyrenees
photo.credit: <!-- photo: ordesa-valley-pyrenees.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Valley of Ordesa, Ordesa y Monte Perdido National Park, Spain.jpg" by Heparina1985 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Valley_of_Ordesa,_Ordesa_y_Monte_Perdido_National_Park,_Spain.jpg -->

meta: One of Spain's Oldest National Parks

entries.name: Swiss National Park
entries.desc: The oldest national park in the Alps, a strict nature reserve in the Engadine where human intervention is minimal and ibex, chamois, and marmots roam freely.

photo.alt: Alpine valley and peaks of the Swiss National Park in the Engadine
photo.credit: <!-- photo: swiss-national-park-engadine.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Swiss National Park 045.JPG" by Hansueli Krapf · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Swiss_National_Park_045.JPG -->

meta: Oldest National Park in the Alps

entries.name: Serengeti National Park
entries.desc: A UNESCO site of vast savanna plains, best known for the annual Great Migration of wildebeest and zebra and for dense populations of lions, leopards, and cheetahs.

photo.alt: Wildebeest herds crossing the Serengeti savanna during the Great Migration
photo.credit: <!-- photo: serengeti-wildebeest-migration.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 4.0 · "Wildebeest Migration in Serengeti National Park, Tanzania.jpg" by Daniel Rosengren · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Wildebeest_Migration_in_Serengeti_National_Park,_Tanzania.jpg -->

entries.name: Kilimanjaro National Park
entries.desc: Protects Mount Kilimanjaro, Africa's highest peak and the world's tallest free-standing mountain, whose routes to the summit pass through rainforest, moorland, and alpine desert.

photo.alt: Snow-capped summit of Mount Kilimanjaro rising above the surrounding plains
photo.credit: <!-- photo: mount-kilimanjaro.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Mt. Kilimanjaro 12.2006.jpg" by Chris 73 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mt._Kilimanjaro_12.2006.jpg -->

entries.name: Ngorongoro Conservation Area
entries.desc: A UNESCO site built around the world's largest intact volcanic caldera, which acts as a natural enclosure for one of the densest concentrations of wildlife on Earth — all Big Five species inhabit the crater floor.

photo.alt: The Ngorongoro Crater floor seen from the caldera rim
photo.credit: <!-- photo: ngorongoro-crater.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Ngorongoro Crater.jpg" by William Warby from London, England · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Ngorongoro_Crater.jpg -->

entries.name: Lake District National Park
entries.desc: England's largest national park, a UNESCO landscape of ribbon lakes and rounded fells including Scafell Pike, long associated with Wordsworth and the Romantic poets.

photo.alt: Lake Windermere framed by the fells of the Lake District
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-windermere.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Lake windermere in 2005.jpg" by Jmstylr · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lake_windermere_in_2005.jpg -->

entries.name: Cairngorms National Park
entries.desc: The largest national park in the United Kingdom, centred on a high sub-arctic plateau of ancient Caledonian pine forest, moorland, and Scotland's tallest mountains after Ben Nevis.

photo.alt: Ancient Caledonian pine forest and moorland in the Cairngorms
photo.credit: <!-- photo: cairngorms-national-park.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Cairngorms National Park, Scotland" by Thomas Andy Branson · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cairngorms_National_Park,_Scotland.jpg -->

entries.name: Denali National Park
entries.desc: Built around Denali, the highest peak in North America, amid vast subarctic wilderness. A single road penetrates the interior, home to grizzly bears, caribou, moose, wolves, and Dall sheep.

photo.alt: Denali reflected in Wonder Lake
photo.credit: <!-- photo: denali-wonder-lake.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "Wonder Lake and Denali" by Denali National Park and Preserve (NPS) · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Wonder_Lake_and_Denali.jpg -->

entries.name: Kenai Fjords National Park
entries.desc: Centered on the Harding Icefield, one of the largest icefields in the United States, with tidewater glaciers calving into glacier-carved fjords. Boat tours access marine wildlife including orcas, sea otters, and millions of seabirds.

photo.alt: Tidewater glacier calving into a fjord in Kenai Fjords National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: kenai-fjords-northwestern-glacier.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Kenai Fjords National Park, Alaska - Northwest Glacier" by CROCKERBD · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:KENAI_FJORDS_NATIONAL_PARK,_ALASKA_-_NORTHWEST_GLACIER.jpg -->

entries.name: Grand Canyon National Park
entries.desc: A mile-deep gorge carved by the Colorado River over millions of years, exposing layered rock spanning much of Earth's history. Rim viewpoints and inner-canyon trails reveal its vast scale.

photo.alt: View into the Grand Canyon from Mather Point
photo.credit: <!-- photo: grand-canyon-mather-point.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "View from Mather Point, Grand Canyon" by Supercarwaar · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:View_from_Mather_Point,_Grand_Canyon.jpg -->

entries.name: Saguaro National Park
entries.desc: Protects dense stands of the giant saguaro cactus, an icon of the Sonoran Desert, in two sections flanking Tucson. The desert supports diverse plant and animal life adapted to arid conditions.

photo.alt: Saguaro cactus forest in the Sonoran Desert
photo.credit: <!-- photo: saguaro-cactus-forest.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "Saguaro cactus forest" by NPS / Todd M. Edgar · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Saguaro_cactus_forest.jpg -->

entries.name: Death Valley National Park
entries.desc: The hottest, driest, and lowest national park, with Badwater Basin lying below sea level. A landscape of salt flats, sand dunes, and colorful eroded badlands.

photo.alt: Salt flats at Badwater Basin, Death Valley
photo.credit: <!-- photo: death-valley-badwater-basin.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Badwater Basin - Death Valley, California" by Julietvbarbara · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Badwater_Basin_-_Death_Valley,_California.JPG -->

entries.name: Joshua Tree National Park
entries.desc: Where the Mojave and Colorado deserts meet, named for its twisted Joshua trees. Massive granite boulder piles draw rock climbers, and dark skies make it a noted stargazing destination.

photo.alt: Joshua trees and granite boulders in Joshua Tree National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: joshua-tree-national-park.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Joshua-National-Park-California0965" by Diego Delso · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Joshua-National-Park-California0965.JPG -->

entries.name: Redwood National Park
entries.desc: Protects old-growth coast redwoods, the tallest trees on Earth, along the northern California coast. Managed jointly with adjoining state parks, it also shelters prairie, rivers, and shoreline.

photo.alt: Old-growth coast redwood forest in Prairie Creek Redwoods State Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: redwood-prairie-creek-forest.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "Prairie Creek Redwoods State Park" by California Department of Parks and Recreation · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Prairie_Creek_Redwoods_State_Park.jpg -->

entries.name: Sequoia National Park
entries.desc: Home to General Sherman, the world's largest tree by volume, and groves of ancient giant sequoias. The park extends into the High Sierra above 14,000 feet at Mount Whitney, the highest summit in the contiguous United States.

photo.alt: General Sherman Tree, the world's largest tree by volume, in Sequoia National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: general-sherman-tree.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "The General Sherman tree - Sequoia National Park (33241877800).jpg" by m01229 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_General_Sherman_tree_-_Sequoia_National_Park_(33241877800).jpg -->

entries.name: Yosemite National Park
entries.desc: A Sierra Nevada park famed for sheer granite formations like El Capitan and Half Dome, tall waterfalls, and groves of giant sequoias. A cornerstone of the early conservation movement.

photo.alt: El Capitan, Half Dome, and Bridalveil Fall in Yosemite Valley, seen from Tunnel View
photo.credit: <!-- photo: yosemite-valley-tunnel-view.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Yosemite National Park, El Capitan, Half Dome, and Bridalveil Fall, from Tunnel View (7882617076).jpg" by Arian Zwegers · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Yosemite_National_Park,_El_Capitan,_Half_Dome,_and_Bridalveil_Fall,_from_Tunnel_View_(7882617076).jpg -->

entries.name: Great Sand Dunes National Park
entries.desc: Contains the tallest sand dunes in North America, piled against the Sangre de Cristo Mountains. Seasonal Medano Creek runs at the dunes' base, and sand sledding is popular.

photo.alt: Tallest sand dunes in North America at Great Sand Dunes National Park, backed by the Sangre de Cristo Mountains
photo.credit: <!-- photo: great-sand-dunes.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Great Sand Dunes NP 1.JPG" by Preiselbeere · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Great_Sand_Dunes_NP_1.JPG -->

entries.name: Mesa Verde National Park
entries.desc: A UNESCO site preserving the most notable concentration of Ancestral Puebloan cliff dwellings in North America, including Cliff Palace, built into the alcoves of sandstone canyons around 1200 CE.

photo.alt: Cliff Palace, the largest Ancestral Puebloan cliff dwelling, in Mesa Verde National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: cliff-palace-mesa-verde.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Cliff Palace-Colorado-Mesa Verde NP.jpg" by Tobi 87 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cliff_Palace-Colorado-Mesa_Verde_NP.jpg -->

entries.name: Rocky Mountain National Park
entries.desc: Straddles the Continental Divide with dozens of peaks above 12,000 feet and broad alpine tundra. Trail Ridge Road, among the highest paved roads in the country, crosses it. Elk are abundant.

photo.alt: Trail Ridge Road and Longs Peak in Rocky Mountain National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: trail-ridge-road-longs-peak.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Trail Ridge Road and Longs Peak by RO.jpg" by Rationalobserver · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Trail_Ridge_Road_and_Longs_Peak_by_RO.jpg -->

meta: Among Highest Paved Roads in the US

entries.name: Everglades National Park
entries.desc: A vast subtropical wetland of sawgrass marsh, mangroves, and slow-moving water at the tip of Florida. A refuge for alligators, crocodiles, manatees, and wading birds, and a designated wilderness.

photo.alt: Sawgrass marsh and wetlands of Everglades National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: everglades-sawgrass-prairie.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "Everglades Sawgrass Prairie Moni3.JPG" by Moni3 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Everglades_Sawgrass_Prairie_Moni3.JPG -->

entries.name: Hawaii Volcanoes National Park
entries.desc: Encompasses two of the world's most active volcanoes, Kilauea and Mauna Loa, on the Big Island. Visitors can see lava fields, craters, steam vents, and lava tubes across shifting terrain.

photo.alt: Kīlauea Caldera in Hawaiʻi Volcanoes National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: kilauea-caldera.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Kīlauea Caldera, Hawaiʻi Volcanoes National Park, Hawaii (4528685941).jpg" by Ken Lund · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:K%C4%ABlauea_Caldera,_Hawai%CA%BBi_Volcanoes_National_Park,_Hawaii_(4528685941).jpg -->

entries.name: Acadia National Park
entries.desc: Set mostly on Mount Desert Island, where granite peaks meet the rocky Atlantic coast. Cadillac Mountain is among the first places to see sunrise in the United States. Historic carriage roads wind throughout.

photo.alt: Sunrise view from Cadillac Mountain in Acadia National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: cadillac-mountain-sunrise.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Cadillac Mountain Sunrise, Acadia National Park - 51651027265.jpg" by John Manard · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cadillac_Mountain_Sunrise,_Acadia_National_Park_-_51651027265.jpg -->

entries.name: Glacier National Park
entries.desc: A rugged expanse of glacier-carved peaks, alpine lakes, and remaining ice fields along the Continental Divide. The Going-to-the-Sun Road crosses the park through dramatic mountain scenery.

photo.alt: Going-to-the-Sun Road winding through Glacier National Park's mountains
photo.credit: <!-- photo: going-to-the-sun-road-glacier.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Going-to-the-Sun Road - Glacier National Park.jpg" by MPSharwood · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Going-to-the-Sun_Road_-_Glacier_National_Park.jpg -->

entries.name: Crater Lake National Park
entries.desc: Built around a volcanic caldera holding the deepest lake in the United States, renowned for its striking deep-blue color and exceptional clarity. Wizard Island rises from the lake's surface as a smaller volcanic cone.

photo.alt: Crater Lake and Wizard Island volcanic cone
photo.credit: <!-- photo: crater-lake-wizard-island.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Crater Lake and Wizard Island.jpg" by Spinner789 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Crater_Lake_and_Wizard_Island.jpg -->

entries.name: Badlands National Park
entries.desc: A landscape of sharply eroded buttes, pinnacles, and spires rising from mixed-grass prairie, with one of the world's richest fossil beds from ancient mammals. Bison, bighorn sheep, and black-footed ferrets roam the park.

photo.alt: Eroded buttes and spires of the White River Badlands, South Dakota
photo.credit: <!-- photo: white-river-badlands-south-dakota.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "White River Badlands of South Dakota panorama.jpg" by James St. John · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:White_River_Badlands_of_South_Dakota_panorama.jpg -->

entries.name: Great Smoky Mountains National Park
entries.desc: The most visited national park, spanning the forested Appalachian ridges named for their bluish haze. Exceptional biodiversity, historic Appalachian structures, and synchronous fireflies distinguish it.

photo.alt: Forested ridges of Great Smoky Mountains National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: great-smoky-mountains.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Great Smoky Mountains National Park 01.jpg" by Niagara66 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Great_Smoky_Mountains_National_Park_01.jpg -->

entries.name: Big Bend National Park
entries.desc: Where the Chihuahuan Desert meets the Rio Grande at the Mexican border, with towering limestone canyons, the Chisos Mountains rising from the desert floor, and some of the darkest night skies in the lower 48.

photo.alt: Chisos Mountains rising from the desert floor in Big Bend National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: big-bend-chisos-mountains.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "Gfp-texas-big-bend-national-park-rocky-mountain-side.jpg" by Yinan Chen · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Gfp-texas-big-bend-national-park-rocky-mountain-side.jpg -->

entries.name: Arches National Park
entries.desc: Home to over two thousand natural sandstone arches, the greatest such density in the world, alongside balanced rocks and fins. Delicate Arch is its best-known formation.

photo.alt: Delicate Arch sandstone formation in Arches National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: delicate-arch-arches.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Delicate Arch - Arches National Park Utah.jpg" by Tatibitati · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Delicate_Arch_-_Arches_National_Park_Utah.jpg -->

entries.name: Bryce Canyon National Park
entries.desc: Known for its dense concentration of hoodoos, spire-shaped rock formations eroded from the edge of a high plateau. Its high elevation and clear air make it excellent for stargazing.

photo.alt: Hoodoos of the Bryce Canyon amphitheater
photo.credit: <!-- photo: bryce-canyon-hoodoos-amphitheater.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Bryce Canyon Amphitheater Hoodoos Panorama.jpg" by Jon Zander (digon3) · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bryce_Canyon_Amphitheater_Hoodoos_Panorama.jpg -->

entries.name: Canyonlands National Park
entries.desc: A vast desert wilderness of canyons, mesas, and buttes carved by the Colorado and Green rivers. Divided into distinct districts, it offers expansive overlooks and remote backcountry.

photo.alt: View from Green River Overlook across Canyonlands National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: canyonlands-green-river-overlook.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "View from Green River Overlook in Canyonlands NP.jpg" by Aneta Kaluzna · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:View_from_Green_River_Overlook_in_Canyonlands_NP.jpg -->

entries.name: Capitol Reef National Park
entries.desc: Centered on the Waterpocket Fold, a nearly 100-mile wrinkle in the Earth's crust exposing layers of colorful sandstone. Less crowded than Utah's other parks, it combines dramatic canyon hiking with a historic Fremont-era fruit orchard.

photo.alt: The Waterpocket Fold's colorful sandstone layers in Capitol Reef National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: capitol-reef-waterpocket-fold.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "2008-0914-CapitolReef-WaterpocketFold1.jpg" by Bobak Ha'Eri · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:2008-0914-CapitolReef-WaterpocketFold1.jpg -->

entries.name: Zion National Park
entries.desc: Centered on a deep sandstone canyon cut by the Virgin River, with towering red and cream cliffs. Signature hikes include Angels Landing and the river-wading route through the Narrows.

photo.alt: The Virgin River winding through Zion Canyon's red sandstone walls
photo.credit: <!-- photo: zion-narrows-virgin-river.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "North Fork Virgin River, Zion Canyon, Zion National Park, Utah (1025001933).jpg" by Ken Lund · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:North_Fork_Virgin_River,_Zion_Canyon,_Zion_National_Park,_Utah_(1025001933).jpg -->

entries.name: Shenandoah National Park
entries.desc: A long, narrow park along the Blue Ridge Mountains, traversed by the scenic Skyline Drive. Known for forested slopes, waterfalls, and vivid autumn foliage, with a stretch of the Appalachian Trail.

photo.alt: Skyline Drive winding along the forested ridge of Shenandoah National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: shenandoah-skyline-drive.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Shenandoah Skyline Drive.JPG" by Ad Meskens · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Shenandoah_Skyline_Drive.JPG -->

entries.name: Mount Rainier National Park
entries.desc: Centered on a glaciated stratovolcano that is the highest peak in the Cascade Range. Subalpine wildflower meadows, ancient forest, and numerous glaciers surround the summit.

photo.alt: Wildflower meadows below Mount Rainier's glaciated summit
photo.credit: <!-- photo: mount-rainier-paradise-meadow.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Mount Rainier - Paradise Meadow - Alta Vista Trail 09.jpg" by Joe Mabel · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mount_Rainier_-_Paradise_Meadow_-_Alta_Vista_Trail_09.jpg -->

entries.name: North Cascades National Park
entries.desc: One of the most rugged and least-visited parks, with more glaciers than any other area in the contiguous United States. Sharp peaks, old-growth forest, and the turquoise waters of the Ross Lake Recreation Area define the landscape.

photo.alt: The turquoise waters of Ross Lake in North Cascades National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: north-cascades-ross-lake.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 4.0 · "North Cascades Natnl Pk Washington State Ross Lake 4311.jpg" by ImagePerson · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:North_Cascades_Natnl_Pk_Washington_State_Ross_Lake_4311.jpg -->

entries.name: Olympic National Park
entries.desc: Spans three distinct ecosystems on the Olympic Peninsula: temperate rainforest, rugged Pacific coastline, and glaciated mountains. The Hoh Rain Forest is among the wettest places in the contiguous United States.

photo.alt: Moss-draped old-growth trees in the Hoh Rain Forest
photo.credit: <!-- photo: olympic-hoh-rain-forest.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "Hoh Rain Forest, Olympic National Park, Washington State, 1992.JPG" by Michael Gäbler · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Hoh_Rain_Forest,_Olympic_National_Park,_Washington_State,_1992.JPG -->

meta: One of the Wettest Places in the US

entries.name: Grand Teton National Park
entries.desc: A range of sharp, glacier-carved granite peaks rising abruptly from the valley floor, with the Snake River and a chain of alpine lakes below. Rich in moose, elk, and bear.

photo.alt: The Teton Range rising above the Snake River
photo.credit: <!-- photo: grand-teton-snake-river-overlook.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Snake River Overlook, Tetons.jpg" by Kristin · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Snake_River_Overlook,_Tetons.jpg -->

entries.name: Yellowstone National Park
entries.desc: The first national park, established in 1872, sitting atop a volcanic hotspot. Known for geothermal features including Old Faithful and the Grand Prismatic Spring, plus bison, wolves, and bears.

photo.alt: Grand Prismatic Spring's rainbow-colored hot spring terraces
photo.credit: <!-- photo: yellowstone-grand-prismatic-spring.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "Grand Prismatic Spring, Yellowstone National Park USA.jpg" by Michael Gäbler · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Grand_Prismatic_Spring,_Yellowstone_National_Park_USA.jpg -->

entries.name: Phong Nha-Ke Bang National Park
entries.desc: A UNESCO karst landscape riddled with caves, including Son Doong, considered the world's largest, set among limestone mountains and underground rivers.

photo.alt: Paradise Cave stalactite and stalagmite formations in Phong Nha-Ke Bang National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: paradise-cave-phong-nha.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Cueva Paraíso, Phong Nha, Vietnam (36277324796).jpg" by Edgardo W. Olivera · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cueva_Para%C3%ADso,_Phong_Nha,_Vietnam_(36277324796).jpg -->
